items.summary: NCDEX has launched RAINMUMBAI, India’s first SEBI-approved exchange-traded weather derivatives contract based on Mumbai rainfall. Built with IIT Bombay and IMD data, it enables stakeholders like farmers, utilities, and lenders to hedge monsoon risks using a transparent, data-driven financial instrument.

The company is committed to reliable supply and consistent quality for Indian patients.

To boost its manufacturing share, India aims to increase R&D expenditure to 2% by 2035, requiring greater private sector involvement and stronger innovation ecosystems. This transition to innovation-driven manufacturing is crucial for long-term global competitiveness.

items.title: FIIs won't return to Indian markets in a hurry; only 3 triggers may bring them back: Amar K Ambani
items.summary: Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) are unlikely to return to Indian equities soon due to structural and cyclical forces, including modest dollar returns and the AI revolution favoring other markets. FIIs may only re-enter if valuations hit rock bottom, IPO activity surges, or global markets become overheated, making India an attractive diversification option.

items.title: Huge potential to boost food, beverages exports to UAE: TPCI
items.summary: India’s food and beverage exports to the UAE are poised for major growth, with current shipments valued at USD 3.6 billion compared to the UAE’s total imports of USD 22 billion. Holding a 10 percent market share, India is emerging as a trusted supplier of quality food products to the Gulf nation. Industry experts believe the India-UAE free trade agreement and the upcoming Indo-Food Park in Mumbai will further strengthen trade ties, enhance export infrastructure, and unlock new opportunities for Indian exporters in the UAE market.

The visit will focus on expanding cooperation in trade, investment, artificial intelligence and the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC), a project aimed at improving trade links between India, the Gulf and Europe.

items.summary: India's automakers urge the government to lower prices of high-ethanol fuels like E85 and E100 to boost consumer adoption of flex-fuel vehicles. The industry seeks tax benefits as part of a strategy to enhance ethanol blending amid rising energy security concerns.

items.title: ICRA sees Q4 growth slowing to three-qtr low of 7%
items.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026. ICRA forecasts a 7% growth rate, down from the previous quarter. This slowdown is attributed to weaker industrial and services sector expansion. Agriculture is expected to see a slight improvement. ICRA also anticipates a full fiscal year 2026 GDP growth of 7.5%.

items.title: Ind-Ra says West Asia tensions, El Nino to drag FY27 growth to 6.7%
items.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.7% in 2026-27. Higher crude oil and food prices, along with West Asia conflict uncertainty, will impact growth. A potential El Nino event could also affect agriculture. India Ratings and Research (Ind-Ra) made this projection. Inflation is expected to be 4.4% in 2026-27.

The UAE's Minister of Foreign Trade highlighted the importance of private sector collaboration in strategic sectors. This follows recent investment commitments worth nearly $5 billion. Bilateral trade is growing strongly, with a target of $200 billion by 2032.

items.summary: India's Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code has seen significant success. By December 2025, over 8,800 corporate insolvency cases were admitted. Creditors have recovered more than Rs 4.11 lakh crore through approved plans. Over 4,000 companies have been rescued. Recent amendments aim to further strengthen the framework and speed up resolutions.

items.title: IBBI issues new guidelines to speed up appointment of insolvency professionals
items.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India has released new rules for insolvency professionals. These guidelines aim to speed up appointments in corporate insolvency cases. Professionals must submit an expression of interest by June 19, 2026. A final list will be sent to adjudicating authorities by June 30, 2026. IPs cannot refuse assignments without valid reasons.

items.title: NS&I to begin contacting victims of lost funds scandal
items.summary: Over 30,000 estates could not be accessed due to an error identifying all of a late customer's NS&I products.

items.title: Iran war to slow down India’s FY27 economic growth: rating agencies
items.summary: Two rating agencies have cut their FY27 GDP forecasts as the West Asia war drives crude oil toward $95–110 a barrel, stokes inflation, and forces fuel price hikes. The government's own target of 7–7.4% growth, set before the war began, now looks out of reach.

items.title: RBI proposes banks to disclose detailed information on capital, risks under Basel Pillar 3
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is introducing new rules for banks under Basel III. Lenders will soon publish detailed information quarterly on capital, leverage, liquidity, and risks. This aims to boost transparency and market understanding. Banks must explain any significant changes in these figures. The new framework starts from the quarter ending September 30, 2026.

items.summary: For FY26, its revenue from operations stood at ₹27,148.4 crore, up 17% on-year, and adjusted net profit grew 15% to ₹5,456.4 crore.

items.summary: The Bengaluru-headquartered upstart is trying to turn diagnostics into an on-demand consumer service. It promises home sample collection in under 60 minutes, and report delivery within six hours. But not everyone is convinced.

items.summary: PepsiCo India reported higher revenue and profit for 2025, aided by double-digit growth in foods, even as unseasonal rains and intensifying competition weighed on beverages.

items.title: India's inflation puzzle: How will fuel price play out
items.summary: India's inflation debate intensifies as wholesale prices surge, driven by fuel costs, while retail inflation remains subdued. A recent fuel price hike is expected to push retail inflation higher, impacting household budgets. The RBI will closely monitor global crude prices, monsoon impact, and the pass-through of wholesale to retail inflation before adjusting interest rates.

items.title: Bharti Airtel launches Priority Postpaid service with 5G slicing
items.summary: Over the past year, slicing based 5G services have been launched in many countries like USA, Singapore, United Kingdom and Malaysia. Airtel’s launch is the first such launch in India.

items.title: India-EU trade pact to help boost domestic auto manufacturing: Senior official
items.summary: India and the European Union have concluded negotiations for a free trade agreement. This pact is expected to attract significant investments into India. Automobile manufacturing in the country will see a boost. Indian exporters will gain access to the EU's value chain. Prices of imported European cars are likely to decrease.

items.title: New High Street crime unit to target gangs fronting shops after BBC investigation
items.summary: It follows BBC News exposing drug gangs, money laundering, immigration crime, and ghost directors linked to shop fronts.

items.title: Zydus Wellness sees more of price-led growth as costs surge
items.summary: Rising input costs and acquisition-led expansion are reshaping the growth strategy at Zydus Wellness. Rising inflation may push it to rely more on price-led growth, signalling broader pressure on consumer-facing firms as the impact of the West Asia war spreads across the economy.

items.title: Sanjeev Sanyal calls for Transparency of Rules Act to empower citizens, businesses
items.summary: Sanjeev Sanyal has pitched for a Transparency of Rules Act that would create a unified digital portal for all government rules and regulations, enabling citizens and businesses to easily access the latest legal and compliance updates. The proposed reform is aimed at improving ease of doing business and ease of living by simplifying regulatory access and reducing confusion. Sanyal also stressed the need for Indian consultancy firms to expand globally and build stronger international competitiveness.

items.title: India notifies standards for petrol with up to 30% ethanol amid energy crisis
items.summary: India is currently implementing E20 fuel adoption nationwide, while industry bodies and biofuel manufacturers have increasingly advocated movement towards E22 and higher blends citing surplus ethanol availability and the need to reduce oil import dependence.

items.title: 2026 CNBC Disruptor 50: See the full list of companies, rankings, and a new leader in the AI race
items.summary: CNBC reveals the 2026 Disruptor 50 list led by an increasingly powerful and highly valued group of AI companies.

items.title: Downside risks to economy have begun materializing as continued West Asia conflict casts shadow over India's trade outlook: Crisil
items.summary: The escalating conflict in West Asia is emerging as a major risk for India’s economy, threatening trade flows, increasing import costs, and putting pressure on growth. Higher crude oil and transportation expenses are expected to widen India’s current account deficit and push inflation upward as businesses pass on rising costs to consumers. Economists warn that prolonged geopolitical tensions could slow GDP growth and strain the country’s external finances.

items.title: Infosys cuts Q4 bonus payouts after up to 100% variable pay in previous quarter: Report
items.summary: Infosys has reduced performance bonus payouts for Q4 FY26 to an average of 70%, down from 85% in Q3. Payouts varied by employee level, reflecting ongoing uncertainty in the global tech sector amid AI advancements that are impacting traditional IT services.

items.title: Gear shift: Bajaj Auto steers KTM closer to profitability in March quarter
items.summary: In the first full quarter since Bajaj Auto took control, the Austrian bikemaker clocked a €5.5 million Ebitda profit on the back of a 70% revenue jump and aggressive cost-cutting following its 2024 bankruptcy.

items.title: ICRA lowers India's FY27 GDP growth estimate to 6.2%
items.summary: Rating agency ICRA has revised India's FY27 GDP growth forecast down to 6.2% from 6.5% due to elevated crude oil prices stemming from the West Asia crisis. For FY26, growth is estimated at 7.5%. Q4 FY26 GDP growth is expected to slow to 7% amid moderating industrial and services sectors, with merchandise exports contracting.

items.title: Software jobs as we know them may soon cease to exist, says Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei
items.summary: As AI tools become more and more capable of writing code, Amodei suggested that there is going to be a fundamental shift in how software is built and paid for.

items.title: India soymeal exports to fall to four-year low on rising prices
items.summary: India's soymeal exports are projected to drop by half to a four-year low in the current year, driven by a 47% price surge over the past month. This makes Indian animal feed uncompetitive against South American supplies, prompting Asian buyers to switch. Tight domestic supplies and strong demand are keeping prices elevated.

items.title: Global uncertainty 'opportunity for faster reform': Piyush Goyal urges industry to leverage FTAs, emerging tech
items.summary: India can use global challenges to speed up reforms and build stronger supply chains. Union Minister Piyush Goyal stated that India aims to be a trusted partner for investment and exports. The country is working towards Viksit Bharat 2047. Businesses must capitalise on new free trade agreements to boost exports and attract investment.

items.title: DND-Jewar Airport high-speed corridor to boost Delhi-NCR connectivity, ease traffic congestion
items.summary: A significant infrastructure project, the DND-Faridabad-Sohna Access Controlled Highway, is rapidly progressing to ease Delhi-NCR traffic and connect to Jewar Airport. This six-lane corridor will improve connectivity to major expressways and reduce travel times, forming a key part of the Centre's Rs 1.25 lakh crore decongestion plan for the region.

items.title: Elon Musk has lost yet another legal battle. Why he'll still keep fighting
items.summary: Musk has lost his high-profile lawsuit against OpenAI, but is not likely to change his aggressive style.

items.title: The $138 bn buffer: India’s most visible export can rescue the rupee
items.summary: India is considering a policy push to accelerate overseas deployment of skilled workers as part of a broader strategy to support the rupee and strengthen remittance inflows amid rising external pressures, including a widening current account deficit and higher oil prices. According to the report, the Ministry of External Affairs has asked the Ministry of Skill Development to speed up placement of workers abroad through government-to-government channels, starting with demand from countries like Israel.

items.title: UK unemployment rate unexpectedly rises
items.summary: The number of UK job vacancies also fell to its lowest level in five years as the initial impact of the Iran war on businesses starts to be seen.

items.title: India very well placed globally on ease of doing business, competitiveness: Union MoS Jitin Prasada
items.summary: India is a leading global destination for investment. The nation offers a strong environment for ease of doing business and international competitiveness. Government reforms like the Jan Vishwas Bill simplify regulations. Initiatives such as Gati Shakti and logistics policies accelerate business growth. Free Trade Agreements attract global investors. Political stability ensures sustained economic progress.

items.title: India’s rising oil imports push trade deficit into risky territory: Crisil
items.summary: India’s oil trade deficit is expected to widen significantly in FY27 due to rising crude oil prices and weakening petroleum exports, according to a report by Crisil. The report warned that India’s heavy dependence on imported crude could put added pressure on the country’s external finances, with the current account deficit also projected to rise as oil-related trade pressures intensify.

items.title: Starbucks Korea sacks CEO over controversial 'Tank Day' promotion

items.summary: The set of 79 million individuals and entities that make up India’s unorganized sector is seeing productivity and real wages stagnate in real terms, amid declining economic influence.

items.title: Oil prices fall 2% as Trump delays planned strike on Iran
items.summary: Crude prices fell over 2% after US President Donald Trump deferred a planned strike on Iran, easing supply fears. Yet Indian fuel prices were hiked again, reflecting lingering global volatility.

items.title: Standard Chartered to cut thousands of roles as AI use increases
items.summary: The UK-headquartered banking giant aims to move some of the effected workers to other roles in the business.
